URL: https://media.geeksforgeeks.org/wp-content/uploads/20221219104058/Monoclonal-Antibody-768.png
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates the process of generating monoclonal antibodies by depicting a mouse receiving an antigen, leading to the creation of B cells and myeloma cells, which form hybridomas that produce monoclonal antibodies.

URL: https://ai2-s2-public.s3.amazonaws.com/figures/2017-08-08/497aa1f4f7358b215ef8b2f433f3888ecbebfd16/4-Figure2-1.png

enriched_points.expanded_content: Model predictive control optimizes production processes in monoclonal antibodies (mAbs), enhancing yield and efficiency through real-time adjustments based on predictive algorithms.
Continuous bioprocessing systems streamline mAb production, reducing downtime and improving overall throughput, which is crucial for meeting increasing market demands.
Implementing advanced control strategies and continuous systems significantly lowers production costs, making mAb therapies more accessible while maintaining quality and compliance.
